# services.cntlm

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.cntlm`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.cntlm.configText` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Verbatim contents of {file}`cntlm.conf`. |
| `services.cntlm.domain` | `string` | Proxy account domain/workgroup name. |
| `services.cntlm.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable cntlm, which starts a local proxy. |
| `services.cntlm.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Additional config appended to the end of the generated {file}`cntlm.conf`. |
| `services.cntlm.netbios_hostname` | `string` | The hostname of your machine. |
| `services.cntlm.noproxy` | `list of string` | A list of domains where the proxy is skipped. |
| `services.cntlm.password` | `string` | Proxy account password. Note: use chmod 0600 on /etc/cntlm.password for security. |
| `services.cntlm.port` | `list of 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Specifies on which ports the cntlm daemon listens. |
| `services.cntlm.proxy` | `list of string` | A list of NTLM/NTLMv2 authenticating HTTP proxies. Parent proxy, which requires authentication. The same as proxy on the command-line, can be used more than once to specify unlimited number of proxies. Should one proxy fail, cntlm automatically moves on to the next one. The connect request fails only if the whole list of proxies is scanned and (for each request) and found to be invalid. Command-line takes precedence over the configuration file. |
| `services.cntlm.username` | `string` | Proxy account name, without the possibility to include domain name ('at' sign is interpreted literally). |
